sources.label: Yahoo Finance - eMemory Q1 2026 earnings call highlights (May 2026, pre-existing news)
Design-Reuse - eMemory Q1 2026 record revenue, Intel 18A PUF collaboration
eMemory IR - Monthly revenue page (no new June release found)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already flagged removal for weak direct CPO/HBM/packaging relevance and post-rerating asymmetry; no new information since June 6 and today's -1.4% drift does not change that setup, so no second move is warranted.
verdicts.bull: Royalty-leveraged 100%-gross-margin IP model with licensing up 58.6% YoY, 3nm AI server CPU wins, and PUF security IP qualifying on Intel 18A gives genuine secular optionality if hardware-security attach rates inflect.
verdicts.neutral: A high-quality compounder, but its bottleneck is logic-NVM/security IP, not CPO or HBM; on this page it is an ecosystem-adjacent watchlist node whose alpha case was already arbitraged by the +83% YTD rerating.
verdicts.bear: At ~$7.6B cap and 83% YTD with no CPO-specific catalyst, the name is owned and rerated; any royalty soft patch or slower PUF adoption compresses a premium multiple with little page-relevant thesis to fall back on.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Monthly revenue releases and new advanced-node (2nm/18A) licensing announcements showing PUF/security attach accelerating in AI silicon.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded post-rerating multiple on a name whose direct CPO/HBM bottleneck relevance is weak, leaving it exposed to derating without page-specific offset.
